channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania Government Proposes 62.3 Trillion Shilling Budget for 2026/27 to Boost Economy
item.description: Tanzania government ha just released the proposed budget for the financial year 2026/27 which is 62.33 trillion shillings, meaning an increase of 10.3 percent from last year’s 57.04 trillion shillings...

item.title: Alberta family loses $40,000 after unknowingly buying a stolen truck from a dealership
item.description: In Alberta, Ashutosh Pathak and his brother purchased a Ford F-150 from Summit Ram, part of Kaizen Auto Group in Ponoka, thinking it was a legitimate vehicle for their families. The purchase, which co...

item.title: Tensions Rise as Iran Deploys Naval Mines in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: On March 12, 2026, U.S. intelligence detected the beginning of Iranian naval mine operations in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical maritime corridor for global oil transport. The following day, the Unit...

item.title: Human Rights Advocates Emphasize Role in Tanzania’s Vision 2050 Implementation
item.description: As Tanzania prepares to start implementing Development Vision 2050 from July 2025, human rights activists are optimistic that this national plan will help push for social justice, gender equality, and...

item.title: 3SC beats Rivers United 2-1 in Ibadan to move 6th on NPFL table
item.description: Shooting Stars Sports Club (3SC) from Ibadan gatz Rivers United FC 2-1 for a rescheduled NPFL Match Day 24 at Lekan Salami Stadium on Thursday. Dis win na big one for 3SC because e show dem get resili...

item.title: US Treasury Secretary Confirms Plans for Navy to Escort Oil Tankers Through Strait of Hormuz Amid Tensions
item.description: The US Treasury Secretary has indicated that the US Navy will soon escort oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz in response to increased Iranian attacks on shipping and energy infrastructure. The S...

item.title: Canada unveils $35 billion plan to modernize northern military and civilian infrastructure
item.description: The Liberal government has revealed a $35-billion plan aimed at modernizing and expanding Canada's military presence in the Far North while also improving civilian infrastructure. The proposal include...

item.title: Masondo wins interdict against Mampeule over defamatory statements
item.description: On 4 March 2026, the Johannesburg High Court delivered a judgment in a defamation case brought by Deputy Finance Minister and Public Investment Corporation chairperson David Masondo against businessma...

item.title: USDA’s Section 515 Program for Rural Affordable Housing Faces Phase-Out
item.description: The U.S. Department of Agriculture’s Section 515 program, established in 1963, has been a cornerstone of affordable rental housing in rural America, helping to finance over 533,000 apartments and town...

item.title: Mother sentenced to life imprisonment for the death of her infant daughter
item.description: Zara Arsalan, 31, has been sentenced to life imprisonment with a minimum term of 19 years for the murder of her two-month-old daughter, Harleen Bains. The tragedy occurred on 23 July 2020 at their hom...

item.title: Kenya’s meat and tea exports hit by Middle East conflict
item.description: Kenya’s agricultural exports, particularly meat and tea, are facing serious disruptions due to the ongoing Iran–Israel conflict. Kenya ships about Sh300 million worth of meat and animal products to th...

item.title: Lebanese-born U.S. Citizen Killed During Attack on Michigan Synagogue
item.description: On March 12, 2026, a violent incident occurred at Temple Israel, a large Reform Jewish synagogue in West Bloomfield, Michigan. The suspect, identified preliminarily as 41-year-old Lebanese-born Americ...

item.title: Australian Government Redirects Fuel to Ensure Domestic Supply During Middle East Tensions
item.description: The Australian government has announced a temporary adjustment to fuel quality standards to increase domestic supply amid panic buying and international supply disruptions. Energy Minister Chris Bowen...

item.title: Nation Media launches AI framework to guide journalists and protect audiences
item.description: Nation Media Group (NMG) last week unveiled a comprehensive Framework for the Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) across all its platforms. The policy is meant to guide NMG journalists on ethical and ...

item.title: Tanzania Reaffirms Commitment to Human Rights and Post-Election Reforms
item.description: Tanzania has once again shown its commitment to protecting human rights, presenting a strong case at a side event during the 61st Session of the United Nations Human Rights Council in Geneva. Assistan...

item.title: Chinese National Detained at Nairobi Airport for Attempted Smuggling of Protected Ants
item.description: At Jomo Kenyatta International Airport, Nairobi, a Chinese citizen named Zhang Kequn was arrested while trying to smuggle over 2,000 queen garden ants out of Kenya. Prosecutors informed the court that...

item.title: Iran-Israel Conflict Escalates as Leaders Vow Continued Fighting and Oil Prices Soar
item.description: The conflict between Iran, Israel, and the United States has intensified, marking its second week with a significant escalation of strikes and threats. Overnight, Iran launched a barrage of missiles t...

item.title: Ongoing Iran Conflict Disrupts Global Oil Supply and Sparks Regional Violence
item.description: The conflict between Iran, Israel, and the United States has escalated into its second week, causing significant regional and global repercussions. Missile strikes in northern Israel have damaged buil...

item.title: The Impact of Showmax's Closure on South Africa’s Film and Television Industry
item.description: The planned closure of Showmax, following the acquisition of MultiChoice by French media giant Canal+, is set to have profound effects on South Africa’s film and television industry. For local creativ...

item.title: Tobi Amusan Set to Compete at Prefontaine Classic in July
item.description: Nigeria’s top female hurdler, Tobi Amusan, dey ready to make her return to international competition. Organisers don officially list her among the headline athletes for the women’s 100m hurdles at the...
